fullArticle:
Unit sales of print books slipped 0.3% in the first six months of 2026—the period ended July 4—compared to the same period a year ago at outlets that report to Circana BookScan. The relatively small slippage was thanks to a strong showing by the adult fiction category, which offset weakness in adult nonfiction.

The adult fiction category saw sale rise 5.7%, and dominated the top 10 overall bestseller list for the first half of the year. Topping the list is the industry’s only million-copy seller so far this year, Theo of Golden by Allen Levi, which sold nearly 1.2 million copies. Andrew Weir’s Project Hail Mary came in at #2, selling more than 829,000 copies thanks in large part to the success of the movie of the same name.
